response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a broadly experienced full-stack engineer with a strong polyglot background, but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not Java or Kotlin. While Java appears in his skills list and was used peripherally at Altom and National Instruments, there is no evidence of deep JVM expertise, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, or meaningful work on IDE internals — all of which are central to this role. His most relevant adjacent experience is writing C/C++ components for NI's LabWindows/CVI IDE, but that is a C IDE and does not translate to JVM tooling. The gap between his demonstrated expertise and the role's core requirements is too wide to bridge at the mid-senior level expected.
strengths: Demonstrated experience contributing to developer tooling, CI/CD infrastructure, and a C-based IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI), showing some familiarity with the domain
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, and open-source project ownership indicate high engineering discipline
Broad systems experience across embedded, high-throughput microservices, and GIS platforms shows ability to work on complex, large-scale codebases
concerns: No Kotlin experience anywhere in the resume — a hard requirement for the role
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience; enterprise Java frameworks are entirely absent from his work history
Primary language focus for the last several years has been TypeScript/Node.js, making a pivot to deep JVM tooling a significant stretch without a ramp-up period

key_factors.factor: No JVM internals or JVM-based IDE work
